body:
Fixing our broken political system can be radically simple...
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58880947/392530084/-2027002553?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA1YTMyNmEtMGVhZi1lZDExLTk5NGQtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=A4mQDZ29pZKfccIf04kYbOxbFHp84bVNAy2db9JBSuo=&emci=7319b756-e8ae-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=b05a326a-0eaf-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)

Peter — _The Washington Post_ just covered Derek's efforts to fix Congress, and we wanted to make sure you had a chance to read it.

Derek proudly chaired the House Select Committee on the Modernization of Congress to fix the broken process and facilitate bipartisan collaboration. The "Fix Congress" Committee proved _(through over 200 bipartisan recommendations!)_ that Democrats and Republicans actually CAN work together.

**[Read an excerpt from the opinion piece below, then chip in whatever you can to support Derek's efforts to make Congress work for the American people.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58880949/392530086/-1919651591?ms=em23_230217_wapo_engg_gq_act___TK_ettx&refcode=em23_230217_wapo_engg_gq_act___TK_ettx&source=em23_230217_wapo_engg_gq_act___TK_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230217_wapo_engg_gq_act___TK_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=9Z8%2FC%2FOgl1Ypuc%2BA4FvHAbyiqGa7NZq8m3Ncor4Qfz8HPXIbz8HQ3CGrUT86X6wYNo6AmE%2BjqSlTaHV9YAIuCBDU11Ge6oHA77c4TPymt420C3Z7JViFhHKiBsn9SdL6X8oqN9kj1g9JnV6mA4xnBHxeT%2BsYa5KJdB%2FbFkTgzcM%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA1YTMyNmEtMGVhZi1lZDExLTk5NGQtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=A4mQDZ29pZKfccIf04kYbOxbFHp84bVNAy2db9JBSuo=)**

* * *
**Opinion: These radically simple changes helped lawmakers actually get things done**

By Amanda Ripley
After the Jan. 6, 2021, invasion of the Capitol, no one would have predicted this kind of [committee], least of all the committee chairman, Rep. Derek Kilmer (D-Wash.). He met with all the committee members, one by one, to ask what they wanted to work on. The answer was, basically: Nothing. Most didn’t think Democrats and Republicans would be able to sit in the same room together, let alone work with each other.

"Some of the conversations were really alarming," Kilmer remembers. One Democrat told him: "I feel like not only was I in a relationship with someone who cheated on me; I was in a relationship with someone who cheated on me with someone who was trying to kill me."

Kilmer, a former management consultant and state legislator, is optimistic and fairly earnest by nature. His office is decorated with "Star Wars" throw pillows and a framed copy of the Rotary Club four-way test ("Is it the truth? Is it fair to all concerned? Will it build goodwill and better friendships? Will it be beneficial to all concerned?"). But after those 11 conversations, even he felt demoralized.

"We're screwed," he told his chief of staff. "We're going to have to do some stuff differently."

**Sometimes, crises make conflicts worse. Other times, they force radical creativity. In this case, Kilmer and his colleagues figured they couldn’t expect to fix Congress if they didn’t start with themselves. So they made a series of blazingly logical changes to their work routines and behaviors that were, in the context of Congress, straight-up radical...**

...So the members of the modernization committee did things differently, on purpose. They started the session with a bipartisan planning retreat, which almost never happens. They hired one bipartisan team of staffers together, rather than separate staffs for Democrats and Republicans. That meant they started with twice as much capacity — and everyone rowing more or less in the same direction. They got a lot done in the 116th Congress, which led their colleagues to vote to extend the committee’s life span into the next Congress. "If all of Congress could operate the way that the modernization committee has, the nation would be in a much better place," Rep. Emanuel Cleaver II (D-Mo.) said in 2020...

"...My big takeaway," Kilmer tells me, "is we need to have these tough conversations with each other." Otherwise, the resentments and blame ferment underground, and they will come out in some other way.

| CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 6 of 7
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
| WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK?
---
| WEEK 6: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE
---
| |
---
| Senator Amanda Chase
---
Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She grew up in Bon Air and has lived most of her life in Chesterfield, since 1979.
She has been rated as one of the most conservative Senators in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA.
She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans.
---
| THE FINAL STRETCH- ONLY ONE MORE WEEK
---
| 2023 SESSION WEEK 6 OVERVIEW

Dear Friends, Things are finally beginning to slow down now that crossover is behind us. Crossover is when the bills that passed the House come to the Senate and vice versa.
Up until crossover we are literally working around the clock either preparing talking points and lining up subject matter experts to speak on bills, or doing regular press interviews to convey those bill ideas to the public.
Now we are simply listening to House bills that mostly have unanimous consent and sending bills to conference committee where conferees from both the House and the Senate work together to finalize the bill language. On average now, we vote up or down on about 40 uncontested bills and 10 contested bills each day. Next week, we will hear those conference reports and vote up or down on any remaining bills.
To give you an idea, almost 600 bills in the House and over 500 in the Senate advanced, and only one bill passed both chambers by Feb. 7. Few good bills passed and those that did aren't exciting.
With only a handful of exceptions, meaningful bills died and only more technical or clerical in nature survived. Bills important to constituents all failed, like election integrity, pushing back against Covid mask and vaccine mandates, restoring 2A rights taken in 2020, parental rights in education and decoupling ourselves with a law passed by Democrats in 2020 to tie Virginia's vehicle emissions standard to California all failed.
The only good news of the week is that we agreed to pass legislation that prohibits countries who are hostile to the United States from purchasing land in Virginia thanks to Republicans and 3 Democrat Senators Joe Morrisey, John Bell and David Marsden who understand the importance of national security. Next week is our final week and we finish up on Saturday.
The session is slated to adjourn on Feb. 25 and reconvene for unfinished business will be on April 12. After that, election season begins in earnest. All 140 General Assembly seats will be up for grabs in November.
We're all up for re-election. That's 140 seats. 100 in the House and 40 in the Senate. All seats are in newly drawn districts in which many incumbent senators will actually face off against fellow incumbents or face retirement.
While I'm thankful that I'm the only incumbent with an address in Senate District 12, both of my opponents Tina Ramirez and Glenn Sturtevant are not explaining to voters that they are running against one of the most conservative Republicans in the Virginia Senate. While I believe the seat belongs to the people, not politicians; it is important that we spend our resources against Democrats, not incumbent Republicans who have not only a proven voting record but a history of securing not just Republican primaries, but winning re-election in a general election, not just once but twice.
Did the lines change? Yes. And now more of Northern Chesterfield is in the district, which helps. My husband and I both grew up on Bon Air just blocks away from each other and still have family in this area to this day. Although my husband and I are 4 years apart in age, we both attended Bon Air Elementary, Robious Middle School and graduated from Monacan High School. We both worked at the Chick-Fil-A at Cloverleaf Mall in the 80s as teenagers, which is where we met.
My husband and I were married at Bon Air United Methodist Church over 30 years ago, which is the same church in which my husband's parents were married in 1963 and his grandparents were married in prior to that. We have deep roots in Bon Air. It was also my father who helped with the engineering of the new edition to the church decades later.
This week we were honored to have Pastor Mike Mayton of Bon Air United Methodist Church pray over the Senate. It was great talking with him and reminiscing about our shared connections at BAUMC. I'm really looking forward to representing Bon Air, my home where I spend most of my childhood and met my husband.
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| VISITORS WHO STOPPED BY THE CAPITOL
---
| | | I had the pleasure of introducing a great family from my district in the Senate today. Mr. Christian Gutierrez, his wife, Laura, and their family, Christina Rose, Samuel, and their youngest daughter Mia.
Mr. Gutierrez moved to the United States in 2001 and has worked to become a US citizen, a business owner in Virginia, and is one of my constituents in Chesterfield.
He operates his own business, called Dreambuilders of Virginia, LLC and he and Laura additionally run an Air B&B. Mr. Gutierrez received his U.S. citizenship in March of 2022.

| In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in [Senate District 12 ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001zgU7gcvT9-xyg_-xNpvq5TE2gvKJztGhg_6jjg1E-8s6XgQ58NveXknjeGlrTmeOb7pNeaKn1XDPpysbY2n4e4Z5PrVkNYGJtUEH19qILwBKjc_dH9T3M9ySDR6joHJqGYvucDCxVq40HeC4oqbWaS2-xJwOgXB27qsMtqGCHIgErfsQe97T30TUqNjXRHuHam6vw3kywCuziz0RlOX-zWlOJTd5e4X8TNTKMke0wqEF7yW5d6uJSWPK5VQcsiwH&c=IS_RVRb7Zj7rwVi08SZrZHvqb4JmZO7TbZseiOPRGI0axXsvcwpQAw==&ch=zS5FPMI-d7JsNB1dsdx0VhWbqmPMV1eGyOiBaqemTuNaRB5DPIS5Nw==) to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election.
Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us.
If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month.
We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us.
We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair.
Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com.

| SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again...
---
| At the Inauguration of the Governor, Tina Ramirez had no idea my aide was sitting right behind her and overheard her conversation.
I was dumbfounded at the tall tales she told to those who were sitting near her. Maybe it's because she knows she doesn't have a record to stand on so she has to tell a tall tale.
This section is dedicated to setting the record straight. Welcome to the first edition of Tina's Tall Tales.
---
|
---
| For the first time ever, Tina announces this week that she's a teacher.
We'd like to know where she taught and when. Or is she simply telling another tale?
---
| You are invited to come to the Capitol
---
| There are links at the bottom of the newsletter that will help you follow various bills, legislators, committees and how to reach my office.
If you'd like to stop by the General Assembly, I encourage you to reach out to my office below. We'd love to have you. If you call in advance we will do our best to find a time that I can personally meet with you.
Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Debbie at 804 393 2715.
We hope to see you soon!

Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us.
If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month.
We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us.
We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. You will want to turn in your notarized forms well before this date, as this is the date we must turn all forms into the chair.
Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com if you have questions or call our campaign cell at 804 380 0009.

clean_body: From the Desk of Senator Chase | | | | | | == | CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 6 of 7 | | | | | | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK? | WEEK 6: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE | | | Senator Amanda Chase Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She grew up in Bon Air and has lived most of her life in Chesterfield, since 1979. She has been rated as one of the most conservative Senators in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA. She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans. | THE FINAL STRETCH- ONLY ONE MORE WEEK | 2023 SESSION WEEK 6 OVERVIEW Dear Friends, Things are finally beginning to slow down now that crossover is behind us. Crossover is when the bills that passed the House come to the Senate and vice versa. Up until crossover we are literally working around the clock either preparing talking points and lining up subject matter experts to speak on bills, or doing regular press interviews to convey those bill ideas to the public. Now we are simply listening to House bills that mostly have unanimous consent and sending bills to conference committee where conferees from both the House and the Senate work together to finalize the bill language. On average now, we vote up or down on about 40 uncontested bills and 10 contested bills each day. Next week, we will hear those conference reports and vote up or down on any remaining bills. To give you an idea, almost 600 bills in the House and over 500 in the Senate advanced, and only one bill passed both chambers by Feb. 7. Few good bills passed and those that did aren't exciting. With only a handful of exceptions, meaningful bills died and only more technical or clerical in nature survived. Bills important to constituents all failed, like election integrity, pushing back against Covid mask and vaccine mandates, restoring 2A rights taken in 2020, parental rights in education and decoupling ourselves with a law passed by Democrats in 2020 to tie Virginia's vehicle emissions standard to California all failed. The only good news of the week is that we agreed to pass legislation that prohibits countries who are hostile to the United States from purchasing land in Virginia thanks to Republicans and 3 Democrat Senators Joe Morrisey, John Bell and David Marsden who understand the importance of national security. Next week is our final week and we finish up on Saturday. The session is slated to adjourn on Feb. 25 and reconvene for unfinished business will be on April 12. After that, election season begins in earnest. All 140 General Assembly seats will be up for grabs in November. We're all up for re-election. That's 140 seats. 100 in the House and 40 in the Senate. All seats are in newly drawn districts in which many incumbent senators will actually face off against fellow incumbents or face retirement. While I'm thankful that I'm the only incumbent with an address in Senate District 12, both of my opponents Tina Ramirez and Glenn Sturtevant are not explaining to voters that they are running against one of the most conservative Republicans in the Virginia Senate. While I believe the seat belongs to the people, not politicians; it is important that we spend our resources against Democrats, not incumbent Republicans who have not only a proven voting record but a history of securing not just Republican primaries, but winning re-election in a general election, not just once but twice. Did the lines change? Yes. And now more of Northern Chesterfield is in the district, which helps. My husband and I both grew up on Bon Air just blocks away from each other and still have family in this area to this day. Although my husband and I are 4 years apart in age, we both attended Bon Air Elementary, Robious Middle School and graduated from Monacan High School. We both worked at the Chick-Fil-A at Cloverleaf Mall in the 80s as teenagers, which is where we met. My husband and I were married at Bon Air United Methodist Church over 30 years ago, which is the same church in which my husband's parents were married in 1963 and his grandparents were married in prior to that. We have deep roots in Bon Air. It was also my father who helped with the engineering of the new edition to the church decades later. This week we were honored to have Pastor Mike Mayton of Bon Air United Methodist Church pray over the Senate. It was great talking with him and reminiscing about our shared connections at BAUMC. I'm really looking forward to representing Bon Air, my home where I spend most of my childhood and met my husband. | | | | | | | VISITORS WHO STOPPED BY THE CAPITOL | | | I had the pleasure of introducing a great family from my district in the Senate today. Mr. Christian Gutierrez, his wife, Laura, and their family, Christina Rose, Samuel, and their youngest daughter Mia. Mr. Gutierrez moved to the United States in 2001 and has worked to become a US citizen, a business owner in Virginia, and is one of my constituents in Chesterfield. He operates his own business, called Dreambuilders of Virginia, LLC and he and Laura additionally run an Air B&B. Mr. Gutierrez received his U.S. citizenship in March of 2022. | | WHERE IS THE 12th SENATE DISTRICT? | The 12th Senatorial District includes most of Chesterfield and all of Colonial Heights. Click on the link below to see a map for each precinct within the district. | | | | == | Please Sign The Petition to Get My Name on the Ballot | == | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in == to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month. We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us. We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com. | DO YOU HAVE A GOOD SIGN LOCATION? | | Mike and I will begin putting up the first round of 4 by 8s tomorrow, Saturday February 10 and continue until all of our 4 by 8s have been placed. Please reach out to Mike if you have a good 4 by 8 location on a main road or want to help put up 4 by 8s at mike@chaseforva.com. Thanks to all who've already provided such great locations on main thoroughfares throughout Chesterfield and Colonial Heights. The primary is Tuesday, June 8. YARD SIGNS AVAILABLE FOR PICKUP: Beginning Wednesday February 15, yard signs are also available for pick up at these location during their business hours: Bob Moate's Sports Shop 10418 Hull Street Road Midlothian, VA 23112 Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30-6pm Friday 9-7pm Saturday 8-5pm Sunday 8-4pm Dance's Sporting Goods 570 Southpark Blvd Colonial Heights, VA 23834 Hours: Weekdays 8am to 6pm Saturday 10-4pm Sunday 12-4pm If you'd like to add your business as a pick up location, please reach out to Mike at mike@chaseforva.com | ENDORSEMENTS | President Donald J Trump Congressman Bob Good Former Congressman Dave Brat Bishop EW Jackson Delegate John McGuire Colonial Heights City Council John Wood Colonial Heights City Council Dr. Laura Poe Amelia County Sheriff Ricky Walker Arizona Senator Sonny Borrelli Pennsylvania Senator and Republican Nominee for Governor Doug Mastriano Former Chesterfield Board of Supervisors Don Sowder Former Chesterfield Commonwealth's Attorney Billy Davenport Dick Heller | SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again... | At the Inauguration of the Governor, Tina Ramirez had no idea my aide was sitting right behind her and overheard her conversation. I was dumbfounded at the tall tales she told to those who were sitting near her. Maybe it's because she knows she doesn't have a record to stand on so she has to tell a tall tale. This section is dedicated to setting the record straight. Welcome to the first edition of Tina's Tall Tales. | | For the first time ever, Tina announces this week that she's a teacher. We'd like to know where she taught and when. Or is she simply telling another tale? | You are invited to come to the Capitol | There are links at the bottom of the newsletter that will help you follow various bills, legislators, committees and how to reach my office. If you'd like to stop by the General Assembly, I encourage you to reach out to my office below. We'd love to have you. If you call in advance we will do our best to find a time that I can personally meet with you. Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Debbie at 804 393 2715. We hope to see you soon! | SAVE THE DATE | TOMMOROW SATURDAY 2/18/23 | | | FEBRUARY 25 | | MARCH 6 | Legislative Update for the 2023 Session | | MARCH 9 | Senate District 12 Candidate Debate | | MARCH 20 AT NOON | | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in == to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month. We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us. We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. You will want to turn in your notarized forms well before this date, as this is the date we must turn all forms into the chair. Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com if you have questions or call our campaign cell at 804 380 0009. | MARCH 31 | | JUNE 20 | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Adam Frisch, who only lost to the loathsome Lauren Boebert by 546 votes, just announced that he’s running against her again in 2024!!
body:
Did you see?
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf0f337de-a119-407e-a3d1-a0dc47d96427/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, **_EXCITING!!_** _**Adam Frisch, who only lost to the loathsome Lauren Boebert by 546 votes, just announced that he’s running against her again in 2024!!**_ **_There's no reason to think Frisch won't beat Boebert if he has the resources he needs, in what will be a presidential year with higher Democratic turnout _****_in Colorado_**** _._** [**_Can you RUSH Adam Frisch $5, $10 or $25 or whatever you can for a HUGE launch week and a great start, making up 547 votes and ridding our Capitol of seditionist Lauren Boebert?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6d521bd0-4e9a-4094-adcb-f5bcf3b6fd19/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) From his announcement on Twitter: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T60830c63-6121-4b54-83d7-fae03a4a68b2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _**Meanwhile, Boebert either didn't learn a thing from her close shave or is simply unable to learn. August of 2022 her neighbor called 911 because Boebert and her husband--who was arrested in the past for exposing himself to teenage girls at a bowling alley--threatened them.**_ That's a sentence I never imagined having to write.
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tadeeceb7-0dc4-4de5-97ae-278e3450b09f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| **_Boebert wants to arrest everyone who investigated Trump, impeach President Biden--just because--and defund Ukraine so Putin can annihilate the country. These are the positions of a fascist. Just because she's stupid, don't let it convince you she's not dangerous. She's both._** [**_Can you RUSH Adam Frisch $5, $10 or $25 or whatever you can for a HUGE launch and great start, making up 547 votes and ridding our Capitol of seditionist Lauren Boebert?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T133b12ee-cda9-48ef-89a7-d1d4e1b20e53/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **This race is so important. We greatly thank you for contributing.** Cliff
---
| | |
---
|
---
| | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T79635b85-28a2-4ac8-882d-67aefcdae246/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | Cliff Schecter President, Blue Amp Action Amplify Good
---|---
| | |
---
| | Follow Us | [](https://twitter.com/intent/follow?screen_name=cliffschecter)| [](https://linkedin.com/in/cliffschecter-0873a448/)
---|---
| **Blue Amp Strategies**
1776 Mentor Ave. #411 | Norwood, Ohio 45212
513.438.0171 | info@blueampaction.com
---
| Having trouble viewing this email? [View it in your web browser](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/frischannounceschallengetoboebert3-n?wvpId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7e63d23a-a0d3-410e-aaf9-6e97d6f241c8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---

---
| It's undeniable, Peter \- Joe Biden is a President in decline.
---
| But unless the Deep State takes him out over classified documents, it seriously looks like Democrats are going to roll with Sleepy Joe for another term!
They'd rather stick with this Addled President over the trainwreck of a VP who was chosen for diversity points...
We want to know what YOU think, though - and that's why we've identified you as a potential respondent in this brief Focus Group of the America First Base.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Supervisor=20David=20Canepa?=
subject: RSVP Today! Caffeinate and Conversate with Canepa on Feb. 18th. in SSF at Philz Coffee
body:
| | | |
---
| | | Please join me and some special guest speakers for our Caffeinate and Conversate with Canepa monthly events on Saturday, February 18 at Philz Coffee in Westborough Square in South San Francisco from 10 a.m. to noon.

This month’s topic is about mental health, a timely issue considering the mass shooting in Half Moon Bay and for those residents who were severely impacted by the devastating storms that caused significant damage from flooding, mudslides, power outages and downed trees.

We will hear about the resources the county provides related to mental health, including behavioral health counseling and learn about nonprofit advocates that help to improve mental health services throughout our county, reduce the stigma associated with mental illness and support effective treatment and recovery programs.
[**_RSVP NOW!_**](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=caa90f1dd9&e=c0babbc643)
[](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=7af0f07b4e&e=c0babbc643) In friendship, David J. Canepa
---
|

|
---
|
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | Please join me and some special guest speakers for our Caffeinate and Conversate with Canepa monthly events on Saturday, February 18 at Philz Coffee in Westborough Square in South San Francisco from 10 a.m. to noon. This month’s topic is about mental health, a timely issue considering the mass shooting in Half Moon Bay and for those residents who were severely impacted by the devastating storms that caused significant damage from flooding, mudslides, power outages and downed trees. We will hear about the resources the county provides related to mental health, including behavioral health counseling and learn about nonprofit advocates that help to improve mental health services throughout our county, reduce the stigma associated with mental illness and support effective treatment and recovery programs. In friendship, David J. Canepa | | | | | | | Copyright © 2023 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved.

Peter, I can’t believe it’s been over two years since my 100-day election legal battle where corrupt Democrat lawyers tried to hand the 2020 election to my opponent. That was an eye-opening experience for me – **it highlighted just how vulnerable the integrity and security of our elections are**. It’s the reason why I launched the Election Integrity Caucus to defend the sanctity of elections and prevent that same shadiness from happening again.
---
| | **Will You D****onate To Defend Our Elections? **
---
[**YES**](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=4fb7d2e0ce564628aaf3d1187e542575&_e=NLwfVh4jF1Xm8yq3a_VbMzlA_mfcPAKupqFL1IXB-cBkm3B72uIm085DHm_JbV2ilG4ZRxgaajX6wDXkLJiSLThAWAE1u6kSVDoxkXWUYvFk1rt_YlK5suI4rFgqyFuBYqZm5XDNnJ_Smz2SOmYdqdUxNTpq4TJ-g2lYIENIRmuperYgSWoRk3A4qsALKu6_Dp23r6ZR8GzOhKrTYP9EGswgu1ysRsXMPEo3TRKJO9Ybz8sKUX5Ns0ZAOQX4eZHSXcyKf0cPjTBFLZ-ZQcd5EN6YouOjRo0nuyLeXgWGfY3xDDQzjt-QMHsCBCX0JUXhJPOvMSNTMG1s17pqGlZ_26BQyTNvT5jU12S0j_jN3LVMrXRQx4TprjCM4rsUxEhZDuBNTkQD8gF7IBr4x2cFDgOrxJYhYoE-yUwgZtuf1WpJdoa0PXNLXEuf3IvCOD9A2FTYDFET4Rvh9rnA7gpdN2Hw0_xWk9ae5ZDuB1R522w%3D)

---

name: Chairman Mike Gallagher
subject: I’m putting Communist China on notice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
I was just chosen to lead the House GOP's efforts to stand up to China.
|
---
| | | Peter - I'm putting the Chinese Communist Party** **_**[on notice.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62334793:6F28TWGND:m:1:3331421248:47D032D4A9BEE409C7AAD33C31569DA4:r "Peter – I’m putting
Communist China ON NOTICE.")**_

I was just chosen to lead the House GOP's efforts to stand up to China. Up to this point, the CCP has ramped up their efforts to undermine America with no real response from the House.

**Our new Republican Majority will _never_ bend the knee to the Chinese Communist Party, _never_ overlook their human rights violations, and _never_ play Mr. Nice Guy with the communists. It is past time we:**

- Prevent the CCP from buying up American farmland.
- Shut down CCP police stations flying over American soil.
- Kick PLA-affiliated researchers off American universities.
- Expose the CCP's efforts to cover-up the origins of COVID-19.
- Defend American sovereignty.

This is important work for our country and our House Majority has you to thank for it.

_**[Because of your support, we now have the power to get our country back on track and promote American leadership. But our work isn't done. Far from it. That's why I have to ask for a little more support to help defend, grow, and expand our new House Majority. Our major fundraising deadline is just ahead and we could use your support.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62334793:6F28TWGND:m:1:3331421248:47D032D4A9BEE409C7AAD33C31569DA4:r "Because of your support, we now have
the power to get our country back on track and promote American leadership. But our work isn’t done. Far from it. That’s why I have to ask
for a little more support to help defend, grow, and expand our new House Majority. Our major fundraising deadline is just ahead and we could use your
support.")**_

I spoke with Majority Leader Steve Scalise and he confirmed we need to hit our early fundraising goals. If we fall short, it'll set the tone for the entire year.

Would you be willing to step up before the deadline?

The stakes are too high. We must retain the Republican Majority that is allowing us to defend our nation against the Chinese Communist Party and that starts with your help.

Your support can make the difference between a strong Republican Majority or a weak-on-China alternative.

We cannot afford to go back so please join our cause today!

clean_body: I was just chosen to lead the House GOP's efforts to stand up to China. | | | | Peter - I'm putting the Chinese Communist Party "Peter – I’m putting Communist China ON NOTICE." I was just chosen to lead the House GOP's efforts to stand up to China. Up to this point, the CCP has ramped up their efforts to undermine America with no real response from the House. Our new Republican Majority will never bend the knee to the Chinese Communist Party, never overlook their human rights violations, and never play Mr. Nice Guy with the communists. It is past time we: - Prevent the CCP from buying up American farmland. - Shut down CCP police stations flying over American soil. - Kick PLA-affiliated researchers off American universities. - Expose the CCP's efforts to cover-up the origins of COVID-19. - Defend American sovereignty. This is important work for our country and our House Majority has you to thank for it. "Because of your support, we now have the power to get our country back on track and promote American leadership. But our work isn’t done. Far from it. That’s why I have to ask for a little more support to help defend, grow, and expand our new House Majority. Our major fundraising deadline is just ahead and we could use your support." I spoke with Majority Leader Steve Scalise and he confirmed we need to hit our early fundraising goals. If we fall short, it'll set the tone for the entire year. Would you be willing to step up before the deadline? The stakes are too high. We must retain the Republican Majority that is allowing us to defend our nation against the Chinese Communist Party and that starts with your help. Your support can make the difference between a strong Republican Majority or a weak-on-China alternative. subject: Here’s Why the Fentanyl Crisis and Border Crisis are Connected
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DAN CRENSHAW FOR CONGRESS
body:
---
| | | | | | |
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KbdXp645yPGCgKgNLkNgxfuQ9WqbCLFQphHceQCccNaxjofJifnlxOZqyHdWgEysyDXQC_kGXPgFLZp7CmasTaMwYUQ0Ji27aGzWQ7MzK-a4MS55VSy-JfVaXOF6653OPVblPXZEMmv7BBez2sdVQXXqke7KDYCDNG2KiOiiYw85uznDycJvuJyK-ebl5S5M_T26MMptjYp0VxKbRXkLSPcEQIZZb9gLTry1RGrgJ9ScoOFu_wA5Xz25riedbGYeRoBNshMgDD3bjQrm90B7Kj2BxWY0lonG8evlrcnUe6vVgA729a9MEae_Cvui-8zn0tfGClFZUgt6ngHa8Uf0Dg==&c=h9z0hHicg8kwDakUQ64cWqkUT7QmNKeaGb2yfp8CM7HiuhJi4OE4Yg==&ch=pjmtm-Da0hHVDg9fcwDU4zIlc15XPCqQpGTGk7MJyjSfIWWRpTDJzA==)

|
Peter-
Dan Crenshaw here.
The question that has been on the minds of every American these past few days is simple: what is going on in the skies above our country? With the shootdown of unidentified aerial phenomenons (UAPs) in recent days this is an understandable question to ask, but one the Biden Administration is having a difficult time answering. This week, I received a briefing from top military leaders about these objects and I'll share what I learned below. I also went down to the border this week as part of a field hearing the Energy and Commerce Committee held on the fentanyl crisis that is killing tens of thousands of Americans every year. The hearing got heated when one of the Democrat witnesses ridiculously claimed that the fentanyl crisis and the border crisis are completely unrelated, and I made sure to debunk that lie. You can watch the full exchange in the hearing clip I included below.
As always, thank you for your support.
Now, here's your brief:
---
| |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| WHAT IS GOING ON UP THERE?

| |

| Joe Biden seemed to be deliberately avoiding speaking to the nation about the recent shoot-down of "objects" over U.S. airspace in recent weeks. In fact, it took him more than two weeks to publicly address the nation regarding violations of U.S. airspace and all we got from the president was seven minutes of scripted remarks with no questions answered. That is a big mistake.
Scrambling jets to shoot missiles at unknown objects above America is unprecedented and the American people are understandably asking for answers to basic questions. Even if the Biden Administration did not know *exactly* what the capabilities, origin, and purpose of these objects were, they should have explained that to the American people at the outset and assured all of us that they are getting to the bottom of it.
Top military leaders provided a briefing to Members of Congress this week on what they know so far about the objects that were shot down. Here's what I learned:

* We still don't know what these other three objects were. Joe Biden claimed this week that they might be objects from private companies that drifted into commercial air space, but that remains unclear. We're calling them unidentified aerial phenomenons (UAPs) for now. We knew exactly what the China spy balloon was, and immediate steps were taken to stop its data from being sent to China.

* The UAPs were in Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) commercial zones, which means they were flying in the general vicinity and altitudes used by passenger aircraft. These objects had no signals or navigation lights. The first two UAPs were smaller, the size of an ATV, and harder to detect. The third resembled a balloon, and was easier for the radar to pick up. All moved with wind currents, which indicates these objects did not have propulsion devices like propellers or engines.

* Why are we just now seeing them? Heightened surveillance means we're tracking more objects. These objects passed near Department of Defense facilities, and posed a danger to commercial aircraft, which is why they were shot down. This is different from the Chinese surveillance balloon, which posed no threat to civilian aircraft because it was flying above 60,000 feet.

* Newer technology has allowed for better detection in recent years. UAPs weren't detected before because our radars were not adjusted for slow-moving objects. Our radars are designed to track things like incoming missiles, which move at an incredibly faster speed than these objects.

There's obviously still a lot we don't know and in some ways this briefing and Joe Biden's remarks sparked more questions than answers. I'm going to continue pushing for transparency so Americans know what's going on in the skies above their country.

| The fentanyl crisis hits every corner of this country and it's killing Americans -especially young Americans -in every community. In Texas, a man was arrested this week in connection with dealing fentanyl-laced drugs to middle and high school students just north of Dallas. Three students died from fatal overdoses after taking these drugs.
It is hard to wrap your mind around just how pervasive and dangerous this crisis is, but just think about this: The DEA seized enough fentanyl last year to kill every American citizen. And that is just the fentanyl that was seized. It does not include all of the fentanyl that slipped through the cracks and made it into our country.
Make no mistake about it, the fentanyl crisis and the border crisis are connected. Chinese suppliers ship fentanyl precursors to the drug cartels in Mexico. The Mexican drug cartels manufacture the fentanyl and disguise it as candy, common street drugs like oxycontin, or lace it into other illicit narcotics like heroin and cocaine. The cartels take advantage of the chaos on our southern border to traffic this poison across our country and into the hands of drug dealers who then sell it to young people who have no idea what they are taking. The cartels are able to do this with relative ease because Border Patrol and Customs agents are distracted with a surge of illegal immigrants and we simply don't have enough man-power to check every single vehicle passing through a point of entry or block the gaps between these entry points on our border.
Despite the fact that this crisis killed more than 70,000 Americans in one year - more than the entire Vietnam War - Joe Biden refuses to take serious action against the cartels or stop the Chinese from shipping the materials needed to make this poison to Mexico.
I've introduced legislation that would give the administration more tools to target the Mexican drug cartels, but we need to pressure Joe Biden and Congressional Democrats to join my bills so we can get them across the finish line and start to take real action against the cartels.

| House Republicans promised to hold Congressional hearings on the southern border once we took back the majority and that is exactly what we did this week at the Energy and Commerce Committee. I sit on the Energy and Commerce Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations, and we traveled to the Rio Grande Valley this week to hold an oversight hearing on the fentanyl crisis that is killing Americans.
The hearing got heated when one of the Democrat witnesses (a failed leftist candidate for Texas Attorney General) had the audacity to claim that the border crisis and fentanyl crisis aren't linked, insisted the crises are being used by Republicans for political talking points, and even questioned why our hearing was being held at the border at all. The witness was either willfully ignorant or too blinded by her political leanings to recognize this simple truth: the fentanyl crisis is linked to the illegal immigration crisis, and BOTH are fueled by the cartels.
Unfortunately, this is the mindset carried by many on the left including Joe Biden. Remember, during Biden's State of the Union address he mentioned the fentanyl crisis but failed to call out the Mexican drug cartels that are fueling the crisis in the first place. He also refused to acknowledge that the border crisis he created is contributing to fatal fentanyl overdoses all across our nation. And when Democrats in Congress talk about the fentanyl crisis they talk about it like it is a traditional addiction problem that can be solved with traditional addiction treatment. They fail to recognize the root causes of this problem: an illegal immigration crisis and the Mexican drug cartels that are profiting from both human and fentanyl smuggling across our southern border. The reason they do this is because they believe that acknowledging the root causes will implicate the left's policy failures at the southern border. If they acknowledge the root causes, they cannot afford to ignore it and must join Republicans in our efforts to both control the southern border and take on the Mexican drug cartels.
Americans know that the fentanyl crisis is fueled in part by the illegal immigration crisis. This is common sense. Solving this problem should not be partisan. This is a common enemy we face as Americans and we must all come to the table to produce solutions that fix our open border and cause pain for the cartels that are killing Americans.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | == | Peter- Dan Crenshaw here. The question that has been on the minds of every American these past few days is simple: what is going on in the skies above our country? With the shootdown of unidentified aerial phenomenons (UAPs) in recent days this is an understandable question to ask, but one the Biden Administration is having a difficult time answering. This week, I received a briefing from top military leaders about these objects and I'll share what I learned below. I also went down to the border this week as part of a field hearing the Energy and Commerce Committee held on the fentanyl crisis that is killing tens of thousands of Americans every year. The hearing got heated when one of the Democrat witnesses ridiculously claimed that the fentanyl crisis and the border crisis are completely unrelated, and I made sure to debunk that lie. You can watch the full exchange in the hearing clip I included below. As always, thank you for your support. Now, here's your brief: | | | | | | | | WHAT IS GOING ON UP THERE? | | | == | == | | | Joe Biden seemed to be deliberately avoiding speaking to the nation about the recent shoot-down of "objects" over U.S. airspace in recent weeks. In fact, it took him more than two weeks to publicly address the nation regarding violations of U.S. airspace and all we got from the president was seven minutes of scripted remarks with no questions answered. That is a big mistake. Scrambling jets to shoot missiles at unknown objects above America is unprecedented and the American people are understandably asking for answers to basic questions. Even if the Biden Administration did not know exactly what the capabilities, origin, and purpose of these objects were, they should have explained that to the American people at the outset and assured all of us that they are getting to the bottom of it. Top military leaders provided a briefing to Members of Congress this week on what they know so far about the objects that were shot down. Here's what I learned: We still don't know what these other three objects were. Joe Biden claimed this week that they might be objects from private companies that drifted into commercial air space, but that remains unclear. We're calling them unidentified aerial phenomenons (UAPs) for now. We knew exactly what the China spy balloon was, and immediate steps were taken to stop its data from being sent to China. The UAPs were in Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) commercial zones, which means they were flying in the general vicinity and altitudes used by passenger aircraft. These objects had no signals or navigation lights. The first two UAPs were smaller, the size of an ATV, and harder to detect. The third resembled a balloon, and was easier for the radar to pick up. All moved with wind currents, which indicates these objects did not have propulsion devices like propellers or engines. Why are we just now seeing them? Heightened surveillance means we're tracking more objects. These objects passed near Department of Defense facilities, and posed a danger to commercial aircraft, which is why they were shot down. This is different from the Chinese surveillance balloon, which posed no threat to civilian aircraft because it was flying above 60,000 feet. Newer technology has allowed for better detection in recent years. UAPs weren't detected before because our radars were not adjusted for slow-moving objects. Our radars are designed to track things like incoming missiles, which move at an incredibly faster speed than these objects. There's obviously still a lot we don't know and in some ways this briefing and Joe Biden's remarks sparked more questions than answers. I'm going to continue pushing for transparency so Americans know what's going on in the skies above their country. | | | | | | | | THE FENTANYL CRISIS AND THE BORDER CRISIS ARE CONNECTED | | | == | == | | | The fentanyl crisis hits every corner of this country and it's killing Americans -especially young Americans -in every community. In Texas, a man was arrested this week in connection with dealing fentanyl-laced drugs to middle and high school students just north of Dallas. Three students died from fatal overdoses after taking these drugs. It is hard to wrap your mind around just how pervasive and dangerous this crisis is, but just think about this: The DEA seized enough fentanyl last year to kill every American citizen. And that is just the fentanyl that was seized. It does not include all of the fentanyl that slipped through the cracks and made it into our country. Make no mistake about it, the fentanyl crisis and the border crisis are connected. Chinese suppliers ship fentanyl precursors to the drug cartels in Mexico. The Mexican drug cartels manufacture the fentanyl and disguise it as candy, common street drugs like oxycontin, or lace it into other illicit narcotics like heroin and cocaine. The cartels take advantage of the chaos on our southern border to traffic this poison across our country and into the hands of drug dealers who then sell it to young people who have no idea what they are taking. The cartels are able to do this with relative ease because Border Patrol and Customs agents are distracted with a surge of illegal immigrants and we simply don't have enough man-power to check every single vehicle passing through a point of entry or block the gaps between these entry points on our border. Despite the fact that this crisis killed more than 70,000 Americans in one year - more than the entire Vietnam War - Joe Biden refuses to take serious action against the cartels or stop the Chinese from shipping the materials needed to make this poison to Mexico. I've introduced legislation that would give the administration more tools to target the Mexican drug cartels, but we need to pressure Joe Biden and Congressional Democrats to join my bills so we can get them across the finish line and start to take real action against the cartels. == | | | | | | | | FENTANYL HEARING AT THE SOUTHERN BORDER | == | == | House Republicans promised to hold Congressional hearings on the southern border once we took back the majority and that is exactly what we did this week at the Energy and Commerce Committee. I sit on the Energy and Commerce Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations, and we traveled to the Rio Grande Valley this week to hold an oversight hearing on the fentanyl crisis that is killing Americans. The hearing got heated when one of the Democrat witnesses (a failed leftist candidate for Texas Attorney General) had the audacity to claim that the border crisis and fentanyl crisis aren't linked, insisted the crises are being used by Republicans for political talking points, and even questioned why our hearing was being held at the border at all. The witness was either willfully ignorant or too blinded by her political leanings to recognize this simple truth: the fentanyl crisis is linked to the illegal immigration crisis, and BOTH are fueled by the cartels. Unfortunately, this is the mindset carried by many on the left including Joe Biden. Remember, during Biden's State of the Union address he mentioned the fentanyl crisis but failed to call out the Mexican drug cartels that are fueling the crisis in the first place. He also refused to acknowledge that the border crisis he created is contributing to fatal fentanyl overdoses all across our nation. And when Democrats in Congress talk about the fentanyl crisis they talk about it like it is a traditional addiction problem that can be solved with traditional addiction treatment. They fail to recognize the root causes of this problem: an illegal immigration crisis and the Mexican drug cartels that are profiting from both human and fentanyl smuggling across our southern border. The reason they do this is because they believe that acknowledging the root causes will implicate the left's policy failures at the southern border. If they acknowledge the root causes, they cannot afford to ignore it and must join Republicans in our efforts to both control the southern border and take on the Mexican drug cartels. Americans know that the fentanyl crisis is fueled in part by the illegal immigration crisis. This is common sense. Solving this problem should not be partisan. This is a common enemy we face as Americans and we must all come to the table to produce solutions that fix our open border and cause pain for the cartels that are killing Americans. | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | | | | | | QUOTE OF THE WEEK | | "Back in the thirties we were told we must collectivize the nation because the people were so poor. Now we are told we must collectivize the nation because the people are so rich." -William F. Buckley | | | | | | | | ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | | | | | | WEEKEND READS WSJ: == FOX NEWS: == NATIONAL REVIEW: == | That's it for this week's campaign brief. Thanks for reading. Want to build the next generation of the conservative movement? == None of this is possible without your support. I promise I won't let you down. | In Service, | | Dan Crenshaw | | | | | == | If you'd prefer to donate by check, please make your check payable and mail to: Dan Crenshaw for Congress, 5900 Memorial Drive, Suite 215, Houston, TX 77007. Any questions, comments, or concerns? Simply reply to this email or give Team Crenshaw a call at 281-888-4016. | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY DAN CRENSHAW FOR CONGRESS | | Dan Crenshaw for Congress | 5900 Memorial Drive, Suite 215, Houston, TX 77007 | | Sent by mailto:dan.crenshaw@crenshawforcongress.com

body:
We elected 48 climate champions, knocked on doors, and traveled across the country to support our candidates.

| We wanted to make sure you didn’t miss Jane’s message about our tremendous progress in 2022.

We knocked on doors, elected 48 climate champions, traveled across the country to support our candidates, _**and we could not have done it without your support.**_

[_**With that in mind, will you contribute $10 to help us grow our program?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882072/392534190/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)

| [CONTRIBUTE ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882073/392534191/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)
---
Read more from Jane below:
* * *

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882074/392534192/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)
As we approach the one year anniversary of the launch of Jane Fonda Climate PAC, I am overwhelmed with gratitude for your support.

It was a busy, full, and very successful first year. I am thrilled with all that Jane Fonda Climate PAC accomplished, but I am also well aware that we still have the struggle of our lives. The science is more clear every year – the climate crisis is human-caused, and any solution requires putting an end to fossil fuels.

But we didn’t need the scientific reports to convince us of the threat we’re facing – heatwaves and droughts, hurricanes and floods, and in my home state of California, the record-setting deadly wildfires.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882075/392534193/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)
| [CONTRIBUTE ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882076/392534194/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)
---
The good news is that now, finally, a majority of the American public is concerned about climate change. But the majority of the American public are not elected leaders. Those with the power to make the change we need consistently lag behind both science and public opinion.

There are some courageous vocal climate champions, but our leaders are not delivering what the science demands. And even the growing segment of climate champions in elected office often only get half of the equation, promoting much-needed clean, renewable energy while continuing to permit oil and gas development, negating much of the benefits of the clean energy revolution.

That’s where our PAC comes in. In 2022, of the 60 candidates we supported in the general election, 42 of them now hold offices up and down the ballot. To maximize our impact, moving forward, we are investing in growing our capacities to increase our ability to recruit and engage our growing supporter community and much more.

_**[That investment is only possible with your support – will you contribute $10 today to continue your support for the Jane Fonda Climate PAC? Every dollar you invest has an impact as we lead the fight against climate change.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882077/392534195/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)**_

We couldn’t have done this without you - and we cannot do what we need to do in 2023, 2024, and beyond without you too. I am under no illusions that electing real climate leaders and then driving the needed change will be easy. But I know that it is the most important thing we must do, and I do believe that together, we _can_ do it. We can win.

Gratefully,
Jane Fonda [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58882083/392534201/931979156?refcode=BLU-JANE-20230217-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhZjRmNWY4LTEzYWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=m23n-WC1K65MQUBS_UQAUMKMpm35X0aDkycX3RvWmrQ=)

The science is more clear every year – the climate crisis is human-caused, and any solution requires putting an end to fossil fuels. But we didn’t need the scientific reports to convince us of the threat we’re facing – heatwaves and droughts, hurricanes and floods, and in my home state of California, the record-setting deadly wildfires. = | = The good news is that now, finally, a majority of the American public is concerned about climate change. But the majority of the American public are not elected leaders. Those with the power to make the change we need consistently lag behind both science and public opinion. There are some courageous vocal climate champions, but our leaders are not delivering what the science demands. And even the growing segment of climate champions in elected office often only get half of the equation, promoting much-needed clean, renewable energy while continuing to permit oil and gas development, negating much of the benefits of the clean energy revolution. That’s where our PAC comes in. In 2022, of the 60 candidates we supported in the general election, 42 of them now hold offices up and down the ballot. To maximize our impact, moving forward, we are investing in growing our capacities to increase our ability to recruit and engage our growing supporter community and much more. = | | | = | = | = | = | = We couldn’t have done this without you - and we cannot do what we need to do in 2023, 2024, and beyond without you too. I am under no illusions that electing real climate leaders and then driving the needed change will be easy. But I know that it is the most important thing we must do, and I do believe that together, we can do it. We can win. Gratefully, Jane Fonda = P.S. | | = Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States If you would like to receive fewer emails, | = = We believe that emails are a crucial way to stay in touch with our top supporters. If you would like to unsubscribe from our emails, please

**My name is John Pierce, and I can beat the FBI in court.**

Right now, I'm defending 30 peaceful, law-abiding Americans who protested the stolen 2020 presidential election at the U.S. Capitol building on Jan.6, 2021.

None of my clients attacked the U.S. Capitol Police or took part in the so-called "riot."

They're just good Americans who exercised their First Amendment rights of free speech and peaceable assembly.

**My clients love our country, our freedoms, and our rights, but they face years of hard time in prison because Joe Biden 's FBI is acting like vicious Soviet secret police, hunting down freedom-loving dissidents.**

Here's what the FBI did to just one of my clients, Lloyd Cruz:

On January 6, Lloyd Cruz walked into the U.S. Capitol building...then walked out. Just like thousands of other patriots did, peacefully and lawfully.

He committed no crimes.

**But the FBI sprang into action, looking to throw Trump-supporters into prison. So they used two unlawful general warrants to collect cell phone location data and digital metadata to identify Lloyd as a potential suspect.**

Even after the FBI identified Lloyd as someone who _may_ have committed _an unspecified crime_ , they had _no probable cause_ to arrest him for _anything_.

So the FBI flew to Missouri to interrogate Lloyd to see if they could find probable cause to charge him with something...and came up with _two potential misdemeanors_!

**Let me tell you, the case against my client, Lloyd Cruz, was launched by one of the worst general warrants in American history. And I suspect many other January 6 defendants face the loss of their freedoms because of these outrageous, illegal actions by the FBI.**

What the FBI did is absolutely terrifying...and if you and I let them get away with it, America is done for.

But there's something you can do about it.

I'm representing Lloyd Cruz, and 29 other patriots in court against the FBI and Department of Justice.

Lloyd's trial is already under way...others will be starting soon.

**I 've entered a historic trial agreement to force the issue of the FBI's illegal general warrants. This can impact the cases of hundreds of January 6 patriots!**

As you may recall, I defended Kyle Rittenouse. So I'm used to fighting for patriots under pressure when their rights and their lives on the line.

And I've picked this fight with the lawless FBI because they must be stopped in court before America goes under.

**[Patriot, will you help me defend Lloyd and my other clients with your very best gift of $25, $50, $100, or more right now?](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FV0HUpvViqWMWXNL6h0nzOg%2F8lga8EsM6E892VKFPueoK8Wg/6/0100018660774251-2d4eee53-c5eb-48ab-8ae9-98f4864d0933-000000/2qYIY9e_E08LZqk9wma43x04244=309)**

These critical cases are extremely expensive, and extremely time-consuming.

I can turn only to patriots like you to help fully fund these critically important defense cases.

[**So please give at least a crucial $25, $50, $100 or more right away.**](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FV0HUpvViqWMWXNL6h0nzOg%2F8lga8EsM6E892VKFPueoK8Wg/7/0100018660774251-2d4eee53-c5eb-48ab-8ae9-98f4864d0933-000000/D44jzOkvsAz2zJEq-kY6fC4LFxo=309)

Lloyd and my other clients are running out of time...so please don't delay.

Thank you!

In Vigilance,

John M. Pierce
Founder and General Counsel
National Constitutional Law Union

**P.S. Patriot, the FBI is destroying the Bill of Rights to get to my clients ...but I can beat them in court with your urgent help. [ Please help me defend Lloyd Cruz and my 29 other patriot clients with your best gift of $25, $50, $100, or more now. ](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FV0HUpvViqWMWXNL6h0nzOg%2F8lga8EsM6E892VKFPueoK8Wg/8/0100018660774251-2d4eee53-c5eb-48ab-8ae9-98f4864d0933-000000/G5zMS_KQMi_Zt1jUsH6q3cxI16k=309) **

President Biden is working to not only protect, but strengthen Social Security and Medicare and lower health care costs. Keep reading to learn what else Democrats have been up to this week → | | |
---
Happy Friday, Peter! President Biden and Democrats have been busy delivering for the American people. Take a look:
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/dSCQfi9FLISmU3ZE3bfPhr4SxeJpEDJYWPaLr4T5WGJJvrqF7xsLK7WFY2OTLP-gbBwzFsl34cbPyT-glrZSX7-iUbgO8E1EIENOJmOnlCHSMKc79wVQ0j-JBs6qnKaYYTVJGrSjlSyC-_ogeyXXu6p47trRikuWDAwUvNJQbjEjeyBh7CLXxme84wECKCTClh9MBWOqdYgE8FFoJR-aExUArO5aY6GUnPKewiSVR3e8MHR7DUToRGytEb5SBcr9UM87iNe8PnkMCWy-3m1kiCwkqosXWO22z5bwkK2QYlafi-KBjEWWBg7dsC9fsk62gGM7ZMhVpzS64sIAGAXO-bVhZK3t1Sp8eu30lmUARR473JE_XED-vwRBjFWHiYdcNo3ZWnP7ZPK4tW_EVAyhlZueP3K8Mbnhdmzuc3Y4Gdfr-X7mt8v8tjId-Y1g3lHsfxJPWs9EXwkaqrwhk8FAkVOC-30tcPZjLUpZMMtD9uoo_ZLdqB0OkiaUzTvjIpVNoHxWopRrmy2-zRUGqNZIlljgBbXgvdQGcHt_j5ddBk6FelV6lceq561npLOsWv9VrvhE80ZnmzihWL0kxCXCsw/3ts/NPW51DurSVOG84KiXOL0SA/h0/UQNzi2BbcDkdfOPfjusKcvyYhnwUN_dkqiA7fPfAgME)
While some Republicans in Congress have demonstrated that they’re hellbent on gutting Social Security and Medicare, President Biden has promised he will veto _any_ legislation that cuts these programs -- and he and Vice President Harris are working to not only protect, but _strengthen_ Social Security and Medicare and lower health care costs. **Keep reading to learn what else Democrats have been up to this week →**

* * *
**CELEBRATING BLACK HISTORY MONTH** This Black History Month, we reflect on the contributions of Black Americans to our nation, from Ida B. Wells to Martin Luther King, Jr. to Rosa Parks, and many more. We remain committed to uplifting Black voices and promoting equality, justice, and economic opportunity for all.
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDE-osrDsyUs4KS0eRiq-k7TGGAE0Jla2Z5PxAGa4E55g8r7gq3uaf_m3lNOPKJAenGpRpvIHZ00s2z0NJgMRSdbnbrmaG8QNqGRvEmqZwgCHyGubM5ZYtgEHHxJH2RR-yfu0pW4290RpBkECgBAF174eEos_qTzH28Dwt5euVNmL8GNK8zlV8iH496ihps49pZ1kud4Qgem_etfo9O_XvScSQHBKBw6zwf9EckaqhcW2TGBnDIEaiiAc5Q1ZXLQVSp2nl1JQ67ZAtUj9Iccyd-RIUasPAb18KEtetmK4xkudqB0FcsNtEMOmAcJdRg_7UheoQR_EAuz_EicJf30G7WjRAoVqyZbp6ekjgXvDXw33PgDeWGrzzgoQYpkjoucTcg/3ts/NPW51DurSVOG84KiXOL0SA/h1/_FKbJ3fDpYThusf2NjWUA7cq7Kgik2jb3m_otgPLsEc)

As we honor members of our party who have made history, like Vice President Kamala Harris, Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and so many others, we hope you’ll join us in recommitting ourselves to combating injustice at its source, ensuring everyone has a seat at the table, and standing up for what is right.

* * *
**REBUILDING THE ECONOMY 💪**
With 12 million jobs created since the Biden-Harris team took office, 517,000 jobs added last month, and the unemployment rate at its lowest in 54 years, it’s no secret that President Biden’s plan to rebuild an economy from the bottom up and middle out is working.
Take a look at how far we’ve come:

[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/dSCQfi9FLISmU3ZE3bfPhtcR_iZ_VWlstoxhOGBkHaHzLDuOgPfcM6rzauQnudKa0LJJXu7PNcZKD3uY0HPlIv52ZXGIj2ZZAxoLTNgXYt1ouaPuAZNM17qHJFLEuXPoA7krREDmXcFbhVmhNB28kXQzkzUfjO62lZKK4Q20VpJTA5G_3b44FVnqHVtM9-oiMwnT17KTy_Rc6EsI39zygISa11Qs5jRfSrl3enM8ojxsfdN1CVL0xBapjnTeeqJ4soyKzB1MBiyVD7HU20fD7dlvltRCle3P8_JCQFb7AGIqG3_A-7MAcfGkMuWPLKieujE9STMNUdydXl-wT6TzqvQXeicfK9CVI_T3PWLY1P2wnDpnbSIVpIWv1qfW-xRWH73Ui7aX5JreGrfTv6Rr4rYd7XA4tW2cN3e67VoZEelb-DYt71agw4Q-s17O-NlJFcYGW1imo5CqWDt37UbFhyZBx4T839EkzQOE_hM8xdJbl1HQeL-pEIocxMEGeghJrdTVZ8r1TRjXBU2xR_zg8Pijd6QQzID9s6AeDKXGXBI8RQ32rqwfCQ8oEPQSSMpRjTMuNkdoeaMoJmLZjXB-LA/3ts/NPW51DurSVOG84KiXOL0SA/h3/unNxU5nSSrofHcr3D5VbAmvwJXWqYJIL3EUEwkYco58)

If you’d like to only receive our most important emails, **[click here.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnk6gVFZ1bAZYY22mWHMkylxoQzeOCU7GO334zVXsArWEaPW3BOb1Tf-EReRwWreeRkZz-wqncEnsWqZuKktHInsjpJb4T9W7L7A3d4337rEuiRlktziBRCP_VUwN9fBnBpf55OxgudGijxr2uI-9hv3qweYF-qxc1OJWgH1VxxcdBMM9rop2RM4vHUOTpgO2366pJ9vzzOw-eQhfXgd3aY0BXtxy3Y57MS4KKkNQv-w1XlIFBl4CeeC7ABvluNgIkP_TnOmcJr3gPWurPh2bSG7DpcmDqa25Ru3aQ3UdCxxxFbRz4-aECZBHELj2ZoEawjPt1smDaZSrVQS5kQzPar4iwVs8Vp1VMdWg3hXMO1WGE4lfJKUzLPtsO_XH6MrEjaLY4ejH9_WpK5s6OMG_SrdEhAUZ8rtovZ5NC0z45jRX5A19YvRKOKPpupV3v2ejtvp4Fm4mLUYoa2uuO56tYkiofs8khgTNya_THoJeQfoBQqkUf69WgCtZCCQNuz_Clo/3ts/NPW51DurSVOG84KiXOL0SA/h19/AMKZeLFIdOhBU8_De79g1akLZBS-0nmtZSaeUoi8Iuc)** If you’d like to unsubscribe from all Democratic HQ emails, you can **[click here.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWalZZNA-6Dgih5xl3OksSSKS5SVgi8cXzG2IVbcTitZvu5l1y2k8NA0OQNDjrjbFfdmAgJM4Dim5vH5y4HzMhVJqMymWCBhuYblQNVTzLYyYdsokahIfJ7QhxZKAI8wMdW0PNroJeva9NJWGBBgHPQSTXGB3tHvUQQaGJVp73oeFADtz1R72ShAuBYoo8D6MsMwyjta_drbH1ujhsuYouqP2a-_zurfSh4VQ-trnwYK3QzIdhmscH6nD-z5EaVFv_0XDYDdsKtG1xVB0wIgI8uGexAlvf96muR-k3iMDlZIk11zv3OcXzWmIksjHwAvl2IzuuNIRGmEqDOSKKm_xVErOs66tOxma5UA7CnjqBPAQilhd1MCGZKI29Fx5RxI2jMVn-2j1fdP1PASlHTLgzY2ZBcZelo-PU8PCQ2_Lbuw0BKDCC2uVrm0DhfM4NI6BKwUAldR-7kjE0pNJ1Ql5kINen1VqyTuLJ6NloGhe-q0WU_QZMnD_4mv70ks-7DPT_JIEdZ5aGJCnkx_OLG5Hh/3ts/NPW51DurSVOG84KiXOL0SA/h20/WSWdz0D3Xxs-5fZptLN8x0yIkDuxt5mpLdWi0r50daE)**

clean_body: President Biden is working to not only protect, but strengthen Social Security and Medicare and lower health care costs. Keep reading to learn what else Democrats have been up to this week → | | | Happy Friday, Peter! President Biden and Democrats have been busy delivering for the American people. Take a look: While some Republicans in Congress have demonstrated that they’re hellbent on gutting Social Security and Medicare, President Biden has promised he will veto any legislation that cuts these programs -- and he and Vice President Harris are working to not only protect, but strengthen Social Security and Medicare and lower health care costs. Keep reading to learn what else Democrats have been up to this week → CELEBRATING BLACK HISTORY MONTH This Black History Month, we reflect on the contributions of Black Americans to our nation, from Ida B. Wells to Martin Luther King, Jr. to Rosa Parks, and many more. We remain committed to uplifting Black voices and promoting equality, justice, and economic opportunity for all. | | | As we honor members of our party who have made history, like Vice President Kamala Harris, Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and so many others, we hope you’ll join us in recommitting ourselves to combating injustice at its source, ensuring everyone has a seat at the table, and standing up for what is right. REBUILDING THE ECONOMY 💪 With 12 million jobs created since the Biden-Harris team took office, 517,000 jobs added last month, and the unemployment rate at its lowest in 54 years, it’s no secret that President Biden’s plan to rebuild an economy from the bottom up and middle out is working. Take a look at how far we’ve come: | | CHECK OUT THESE ITEMS: The Official Democratic Store is celebrating Black History Month in style -- and we wanted to make sure you saw these items, Peter. These designs feature sayings from the late Representative John Lewis and DNC Chair Jaime Harrison which serve as reminders to honor the contributions and achievements made by Black Americans this month and every month. Check them out: | | INVESTING IN DEMOCRATS EARLY With elections coming up in 2023, it’s crucial to keep building on the progress and party infrastructure we’ve built together. The Democratic National Committee is the only party organization that elects Democrats from the school board to the Senate and we’re relying on the early support of this grassroots team to power critical investments in voter protection, organizing, and state-of-the-art data and technology. We'll talk to you next week! Team Joe | ---|--- | |

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: “Oh, she’s dumb, she’s just a bartender.”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
This is what they do when they can’t engage your argument.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230217-3905-header&t=1&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)
If you’re advocating for something, or even trying to blow the whistle on something, and you notice that people start to attack your character: it means you’re winning.
This happens to me all the time at work. **I notice on the days when I’m particularly effective, all of a sudden the attacks about my intelligence, my work ethic, and my integrity all get rolled out** — because they can’t engage my argument.
And so, instead of engaging the message, they engage the messenger.
What they’ll say is, **“Oh, she’s dumb, she’s just a bartender. She’s just this. She’s just that,”** in order to get the listener to conclude: “I should not trust what she is saying _just because_ she’s the one saying it.”
**That's how they get people to stop paying attention** to the actual, factual, explosive exposés and things that are being revealed. Their goal is that if you can repeat defamatory statements about someone enough times, then you can just get people to believe it.
**We’ve all encountered people who try to discredit us and tear us down. But bad-faith attacks won’t stop you, or the movement that we are building together, from pushing forward.**
[If you’re able to support our work, can you please chip in $3 or anything you can afford right now to keep our movement strong? Your donation sustains our movement and keeps our team of dedicated organizers doing their work for a dignified wage. Make your donation here today:](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230217.-8TFZO&refcode=em20230217-3905-P_FND_ANO_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230217.-8TFZO&refcode=em20230217-3905-P_FND_ANO_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230217?refcode=em20230217-3905-P_FND_ANO_OPP&t=22&refcode2=3905_2526796_fnsNnq&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230217-3905-P_FND_ANO_OPP&t=23&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=24&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=25&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=26&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=3905%2E2526796%2EfnsNnq)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
